title: MeshCore for LilyGo T5 ePaper S3 Pro

messages.author: dz0ny
messages.text: https://github.com/dz0ny/meshcore-t5-epaper-s3-pro
messages.content_html: https://github.com/dz0ny/meshcore-t5-epaper-s3-pro
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: dbeacon
messages.text: Very promising idea there, thanks for sharing! I just tried it on my T5 epaper S3 pro and it wouldn't finish booting. Programmed it from your web flasher. Any special requirements for the format of the microSD card, or anything? (edited)
messages.content_html: Very promising idea there, thanks for sharing! I just tried it on my T5 epaper S3 pro and it wouldn't finish booting. Programmed it from your web flasher. Any special requirements for the format of the microSD card, or anything? (edited)

messages.author: dz0ny
messages.text: maybe do full flash erase, and fat for sdcard, i used cheap sd card from Temu. You can also copy log from console on flasher page as it has more logs. Also first boot can take a while as ident for mesh is being generated. (edited)
messages.content_html: maybe do full flash erase, and fat for sdcard, i used cheap sd card from Temu. You can also copy log from console on flasher page as it has more logs. Also first boot can take a while as ident for mesh is being generated. (edited)

messages.author: dbeacon
messages.text: Thanks, will try it soon. I tried it a couple times (with a full erase) and waited 10 minutes for boot, so I figured something must have gone awry. (edited)
messages.content_html: Thanks, will try it soon. I tried it a couple times (with a full erase) and waited 10 minutes for boot, so I figured something must have gone awry. (edited)

messages.author: dz0ny
messages.text: 10min is too much, should be around minute max
messages.content_html: 10min is too much, should be around minute max

messages.author: dbeacon
messages.text: FAT32 on the microSD card is fine?
messages.content_html: FAT32 on the microSD card is fine?

messages.author: dz0ny
messages.text: yes
messages.content_html: yes

messages.author: dbeacon
messages.text: log deleted as issue resolved (edited)
messages.content_html: log deleted as issue resolved (edited)

messages.author: dz0ny
messages.text: yep you need to tick flash erase, when flashing
messages.content_html: yep you need to tick flash erase, when flashing

messages.author: dbeacon
messages.text: checked each time.
messages.content_html: checked each time.

messages.author: dbeacon
messages.text: Other images from other web flashers worked fine (ripple, Lilygo's test FW), perhaps I'll try to build from scratch and install from platformIO or similar, but that'll take a bit. (edited)
messages.content_html: Other images from other web flashers worked fine (ripple, Lilygo's test FW), perhaps I'll try to build from scratch and install from platformIO or similar, but that'll take a bit. (edited)

messages.author: dbeacon
messages.text: side note, not a big deal: the link in the readme to the webflasher on github is broken, I think it's the typo of paper vs paepr, but the one in the top right of the page does take you to the webflasher. (edited)
messages.content_html: side note, not a big deal: the link in the readme to the webflasher on github is broken, I think it's the typo of paper vs paepr, but the one in the top right of the page does take you to the webflasher. (edited)

messages.author: pelgraine
messages.text: I ended up getting Claude to build a Meck port for the T5S3 Pro because Ripple wouldn't work on the variant without GPS. Any chance you'd make a version of your firmware for the non-GPS version of the T5S3? Looks really sleek!
messages.content_html: I ended up getting Claude to build a Meck port for the T5S3 Pro because Ripple wouldn't work on the variant without GPS. Any chance you'd make a version of your firmware for the non-GPS version of the T5S3? Looks really sleek!

messages.author: pelgraine
messages.text: Also, does yours have/can you put in cardKB support? Would be neat if we could make it another proper standalone Meshcore alternative
messages.content_html: Also, does yours have/can you put in cardKB support? Would be neat if we could make it another proper standalone Meshcore alternative
